verdict: For a ~5M-parameter template-trained model this is a solid, reliable result: every one of the 24 samples is a grammatical, on-topic, warmly professional appreciation that explicitly names the requested quality and stays entirely wholesome. The clear ceiling is variety and depth — the model recombines a small fixed pool of openers, impact clauses, and closers, leaning heavily on a few stock phrases.
weaknesses: Within a single sampling draw, one impact clause ('it made the deadline reachable') dominated (9/24); over the larger 200-sample benchmark all 16 impacts appear, but the distribution is skewed.
Impacts are quality-agnostic, so some mismatch the named quality (e.g. 'your kindness. it made the deadline reachable.').
Small closed phrase inventory (5 openers, 16 impacts, 6 closers) — structure is near-identical across outputs.
Raw model text is lowercase (the web app prettifies for display).
note: This is an LLM-as-judge qualitative benchmark, recorded honestly alongside the deterministic Python metrics in benchmarks.json. It is not a capability-class claim.
